meaning.en: To enter a state of deep focus and full commitment, cutting out all distractions.
meaning.zh: 进入专注模式 / 全力投入

lines.speaker: Chloe
lines.text: Hey, you still coming to trivia night?

lines.speaker: Marcus
lines.text: Can't. I've got a deployment tomorrow morning — I need to lock in tonight.

lines.speaker: Chloe
lines.text: Again? You've been locking in every night this week.

lines.speaker: Marcus
lines.text: I know, but once I'm locked in, the code just flows. It's honestly kind of addicting.

lines.speaker: Chloe
lines.text: Alright, Mr. Locked In. Just don't crash out before the deadline.

examples.en: Finals are next week — time to lock in.
examples.zh: 下周期末考试，该全力冲刺了。

examples.en: I said I'd lock in at 9pm... it's 11 and I'm still scrolling TikTok.
examples.zh: 我说9点开始专注……现在11点了还在刷TikTok。

examples.en: She's locked in right now. Nothing can break her focus.
examples.zh: 她现在完全专注，什么都打断不了她。

examples.en: Bro, lock in — we've got two hours to finish this.
examples.zh: 兄弟，认真点——我们只有两小时搞定这个。

origin: Originally from everyday English meaning 'to secure in place.' Evolved in sports and gaming culture to mean entering a state of hyper-focus. By 2025–2026, it became mainstream Gen Z slang, popularized on TikTok and social media as a motivational phrase.
tip: 'Lock in' is your mental switch 🔒 — it's about deciding to take something seriously. Use it as a verb ('I need to lock in'), an adjective ('I'm locked in'), or even a command ('Lock in!'). It's temporary focus, not a permanent commitment.
